title: qemu graphical UI shows up when --log-level is debug for podman machine start
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

> qemu UI frontend shows up, it didn't behave like this before, could be because I am using a rolling update distribution and maybe some default behavior was changed.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman machine start podman-machine-default --log-level=debug`


**Describe the results you received:**

This window appears and the output logs are consumed by it.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/867609/174296134-7bddd891-6ac7-4948-9dd4-65adb61a6f46.png)


**Describe the results you expected:**

No qemu ui should display, logs should be streamed back to initiator, just as before.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
4.1.0
```

comments.body:
Could this be caused by a new version of qemu?
@ashley-cui @baude PTAL
I am not sure, maybe this was caused by the /dev/null changes for the qemu process.

@ashley-cui But then, is there an option to prevent it ?

I mean,

* You can spawn the api on demand in server side process, why would it trigger an UI to show up ?
* In a UI tool that starts the machine for the user if not running, you don't want another window showing up just because of verbosity increase of logs.
* (edit) It also makes it difficult to get those useful verbose logs into a report back here, that is good use case for them not to be consumed by the UI

I'd be happy to add a flag that allows the user to change the output and allow them to be written to a file or something like that!  If you want to open an issue on that, I could make it happen! 

However, this issue of log-level=debug defaulting to opening the GUI is intended behavior and not a bug: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/ff06c791ad7fb32b6a055e0d005f7354df43a5d1/pkg/machine/qemu/machine.go#L571
